role: Former Tehran Prosecutor General; member, Death Committee (1988)
bio: Morteza Eshraqi was Tehran's Prosecutor General in 1988 and is one of two officials explicitly named in [Ruhollah Khomeini](/people/ruhollah-khomeini)'s fatwa as responsible for implementing the mass execution of political prisoners in Tehran — an event the UN classifies as crimes against humanity. His deputy, [Ebrahim Raisi](/people/ebrahim-raisi), also sat on the committee. His presence at the committee's 15 August 1988 meeting with Ayatollah Montazeri is captured on audio recordings published in 2016. He subsequently served as a judge on the Supreme Court from 1989 to 1998.
editorial_notes: Birth year not publicly confirmed. Tehran Prosecutor General 1988. Named in Khomeini's fatwa alongside Nayyeri. Supreme Court judge 1989–1998. Voice on published audio from 15 August 1988.
